17 hours ago

Great day out on the doorstep with the team #VoteLabourOut and about campaigning around Redvers Rd area for our fantastic candidate in Darwen East ward Councillor Martin Mccaughran. ... See MoreSee Less

Andy MacNae MP
18 hours ago

Fantastic visit to Sudell Primary School in Darwen 🏫

It is brilliant to see how the school continues to build on its success year after year, backed by the excellent support of Aldridge Education. During my visit, we had some highly productive discussions about a possible expansion of the school's offering, as well as how Darwen’s £20 million Pride in Place funding from central government will help level up and benefit our local community. 💷🏘️

The highlight of the day, however, was definitely facing the Year 6 students! They kept me firmly on my toes with some incredibly sharp and varied questions covering:

📚 SATs

👔 School uniforms

🎓 The wider education system

✈️ Term-time holidays

It’s always a privilege to hear directly from our bright young minds—they certainly didn't hold back. A massive thank you to the staff and students at Sudell Primary for such a warm welcome. ... See MoreSee Less

View Comments likes love 15 Comments: 3 Shares: 2
Share
Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Share on Linked In Share by Email

3 CommentsComment on Facebook

Andy MacNae MP
2 days ago

Rossendale Hospice does outstanding work for families in our community during their most difficult times, but today's visit highlighted the immense financial pressure they are under.

It is vital we step up to help. I have pledged my full support for local fundraising and will be actively backing Hospice UK’s national campaign for fair funding.

Take a look at the Hospice UK campaign and see why this matters so much: www.hospiceuk.org/our-campaigns/fair-funding-for-hospices ... See MoreSee Less

View Comments likes love haha angry 53 Comments: 11 Shares: 8

11 CommentsComment on Facebook

Andy MacNae MP
3 days ago

I convened a roundtable of car insurers, including Aviva, Admiral and Confused.com, to discuss how we can encourage young drivers to drive safely and responsibly, which could in turn make insurance cheaper. Getting this right is vital, as although only 6% of all drivers are under the age of 25, they make up a quarter of all serious collisions. We will be sharing the findings directly with the Government. ... See MoreSee Less

View Comments likes love haha 38 Comments: 23 Shares: 4

23 CommentsComment on Facebook

Andy MacNae MP
4 days ago

Recent reporting has exposed Reform's deeply offensive, racist, and homophobic posts from their local candidate. Instead of suspending him, local party officials have defended this as "freedom of speech."

Today, I wrote to the leadership of Reform to demand action.

Racism, hatred against women and homophobia have no place in our community or our democracy. Read the full report from the Lancashire Telegraph:
🔗 www.lancashiretelegraph.co.uk/news/26040212.blackburn-reform-uk-candidate-offensive-posts-spark-row/ ... See MoreSee Less

Andy MacNae MP
5 days ago

Enough is enough! Whitewell Bottom residents shouldn't have to wait any longer for safer roads. Lancashire County Council promised a speed camera for Burnley Road East two years ago to address serious speeding issues and close calls—and we are still waiting. I have written directly to Lancashire County Council leadership to demand they finally take action and deliver on their promise. ... See MoreSee Less

50 CommentsComment on Facebook

Andy MacNae MP
6 days ago

As Chair of the APPG for Baby Loss and a bereaved parent, I spoke about the undeniable case for urgent action in our maternity services.

We cannot repeat the cycle of recommendations with no real change.

We must address deep, systemic failures and inequalities in the health service.

Maternity safety must be rebuilt from the ground up by listening to individual families and their personal experience. ... See MoreSee Less

Andy MacNae MP
7 days ago

This past week, we celebrated St. George's Day, and as always the highlight is standing with The Scouts, and everyone else, today in Darwen, and last Sunday in Bacup, for the Saint George's Day Parades.

A massive thank you to all those who took part and allowed me to share in the celebrations, and a special thank you to the volunteers who make it all happen week in and week out. ... See MoreSee Less

4 CommentsComment on Facebook

Andy MacNae MP
1 week ago

It was an absolute privilege to join the wonderful community at St. Bartholomew's Church and The Redeemer CE Primary School celebrate their 20th anniversary!

If you are local, their Celebration Weekend is still ongoing.

Today (Saturday): Pop down to the Spring Fair running until 5:00 PM for stalls, a fairground, games, and food.

Tomorrow (Sunday): There is a Celebration Service at 10:00 AM, followed by an afternoon of entertainment and performances from local groups.

A huge thank you to Headteacher Mr Power and Vicar Rev Chris Anderton for such a warm welcome.

Here's to many more years of this brilliant community partnership. ... See MoreSee Less

GET IN TOUCH

As your local MP, I can help you with or offer advice on, such as; benefits, immigration, tax issues, problems with the NHS, child maintenance and education matters.

I would love to hear from you about what you think my priorities should be as your MP and what matters most to you.

Please use the contact form to get in touch with me. Please remember to provide as many details as possible, including your name, address and full details of your issue.

  • 01254 476410 / 01706 489387
  • andy.macnae.mp@parliament.uk

This helps us prevent spam, thank you.

Social Network

Get Involved

Please use the contact form here to get in touch with me and find out more about my campaigns, priorities and how to get involved. Please remember to provide as many details as possible, including your full contact details.
